div1.entryFree: Harlotry, == harlot: he sups to-night with a h. Oth. IV, 2, 239 (Q harlot). Glendower and Capulet use it like baggage as a term of contempt for a silly wench: a peevish self-willed h. H4A III, 1, 199. Rom. IV, 2, 14. Adjectively: he doth it like one of these h. players, H4A II, 4, 437 (== vagabond players? But perhaps Mrs Quickly means quite another thing, as f. i. Herod or hero players).
